rulings.Case Title: PRABHABEN PATEL VS. CARL F. WILLGEROTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 13, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PRABHABEN PATEL'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatory 15.1 is DENIED. The verified statements of Paul and Beverly Wilgeroth in their Supplemental Responses to Form Interrogatories explaining the factual basis for Defendants' general denial substantially complies with the requirements of Form Interrogatory 15.1 Defendants' responses to Form Interrogatory 15.1 are also not required to identify the particular element of each affirmative defense supported by any particular fact. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Health Service Systems Department'S Motion To Compel Deposition, Extend Discovery And Obtain Protective Order Tentative Ruling:
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 13, 2012, Line 18. Defendant Health Service Systems Department'S Motion To Compel Deposition, Extend Discovery And Obtain Protective Order. Granted in large. The trial is continued to June 11, 2012 at 9:30am in department 206 or such other day as the parties mutually agree so that the parties may complete all discovery already served or noticed, to receive the civil service decision, and to make efforts to resolve the case upon receipt of that decision. Discovery cutoff dates will be triggered off the new trial date. There are no discovery priorities, although it is of course reasonable for documents to be produced prior to the taking of a deposition as to which the documents are germane. Both sides, as well as the non-receipt of the civil service decision, have contributed to the delay of the case. Plaintiff must complete her document production immediately but no later than the end of this week. Defendants may take only one more session of plaintiff's deposition, which has already taken too many days. Defendants must provide dates for the depositions of all the defense-related witness that plaintiff wish to depose by the end of this week. Plaintiff must provide full and complete non-evasive response to all outstanding written discovery by the end of this week. Failure to provide responses for a day due to the illness of counsel is not a basis fof the responding party to waive otherwise proper objections. Parties to meet and confer in advance of the hearing as to all of the above in an effort to work out an orderly schedule for the remaining discovery and to minimize further discovery disputes. No sanctions awarded. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed order consistent with the foregoing and the parties' agreements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT DUNCAN et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 13, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS ALBERT DUNCAN and CHRIS KULJIS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to address the following topics: (1) whether non parties to this litigation whose background check information is being sought need to be given an opportunity to object to the production of the documents, See Alch v. Superior Court (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1412, 1427 ("work history information, goes well beyond the information disclosures that courts have found do not amount to serious invasions of privacy"); and (2) whether the requested documents are "letters of reference" within the meaning of Labor Code 1198.5 that would require redaction of identifying information of the authors under Board of Trustees v. Superior Court (1981) 119 Cal.App.3d 516. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 13, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF S.J. DISTRIBUTORS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Answers Of Din W Chan dba Mandarin Villa Restaurant, Without Objection, To Plaintiff's First Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied, without prejudice. No opposition was filed, but the moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. In the event no party contests the tentative ruling, the moving party may, in lieu of appearing at the hearing, prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN MILLER VS. THE MARQUEE HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - General, Set No. One, From Deft The Marquee Homeowners Association And Requests For Sanctions Against Deft And Their Counsel; Memo P/A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 13, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF KEVIN MILLER'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - General, Set No. One, From Defendant The Marquee Homeowners Association And Requests For Sanctions Against Defendantt And Their Counsel. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel further responses is GRANTED. Defendant's objection that the term "Incident" in Form Interrogatory 12.1 is undefined is not well taken. The definition is given in Section 4(a)(1) of the Form Interrogatories - Set One. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Defendant's counter-request for monetary sanctions for failure to meet and confer is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SIMENS & POLO, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. ELIZABETH SHWIFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Elizabeth Shwiff And Shwiff, Levy & Polo, Llp'S Motion To Compel The Taking Of Defendants Depositons; Request For Sanctions; Costs And Attorneys Fees Against The Plaintiffs Moving Party:
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 13, 2012, Line 6, Defendants Elizabeth Shwiff and Shwiff, Levy & Polo, LLP'S Motion To Compel The Taking Of Defendants Depositons; Request For Sanctions; Costs And Attorneys Fees Against The Plaintiffs. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. The motion of Defendants Elizabeth Shwiff and Shwiff, Levy & Polo to compel the deposition of two plaintiffs and the plaintiffs' entity designee (incorrectly styled as a motion to compel the taking of "Defendants" depositions) is GRANTED IN PART. In light of the fact that the court has issued an order to show cause regarding Plaintiffs' request for a preliminary injunction for hearing at 9:30 a.m. on March 23, 2012, the deposition of plaintiff Grace Polo shall begin on March 19th, and the deposition of the PMK for Simens & Polo, LLP shall begin on March 20th. A maximum of three hours of testimony shall be allowed on the first day of each deposition. Thereafter the depositions shall continue day-to-day beginning on a date mutually convenient for the parties after April 17, 2012 until completed. The deposition of plaintiff Mark Simens shall not begin before April 17, 2012. The topics for the first day of the PMK deposition shall be limited to the ten topics identified on pages 3 and the top of page 4 of Plaintiff's Reply in Support of Plaintiffs' Motion for a Protective Order filed March 6, 2012. The documents to be produced on the first day of each deposition shall be limited to the ten topics identified on page four of Plaintiffs' Reply in Support of Plaintiffs' Motion for a Protective Order. Plaintiff shall provide a privilege log of all documents withheld under a claim of privilege. The parties are to meet and confer to limit the scope of the documents to be produced on subsequent days of the PMK deposition and plaintiff Grace Polo's deposition and in connection with Mark Simens' deposition. Counsel for Plaintiff and for Defendant shall each bring a proposed protective order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SIMENS & POLO, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. ELIZABETH SHWIFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 13, 2012, Line 6, PLAINTIFF SIMENS & POLO, LLP, MARK SIEMENS and GRACE POLO'S Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Plaintiffs' motion for a protective order is DENIED IN PART. Plaintiffs provided the court with a courtesy copy of their reply in support of their motion for a protective order, but did not provide courtesy copies of their moving papers and declarations in support as required by San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. In light of the fact that the court has issued an order to show cause regarding Plaintiffs' request for a preliminary injunction for hearing at 9:30 a.m. on March 23, 2012, the deposition of plaintiff Grace Polo shall begin on March 19th, and the deposition of the PMK for Simens & Polo, LLP shall begin on March 20th. A maximum of three hours of testimony shall be allowed on the first day of each deposition. Thereafter the depositions shall continue day-to-day beginning on a date mutually convenient for the parties after April 17, 2012 until completed. The deposition of plaintiff Mark Simens shall not begin before April 17, 2012. The topics for the first day of the PMK deposition shall be limited to the ten topics identified on pages 3 and the top of page 4 of Plaintiff's Reply in Support of Plaintiffs' Motion for a Protective Order filed March 6, 2012. The documents to be produced on the first day of each deposition shall be limited to the ten topics identified on page four of Plaintiffs' Reply in Support of Plaintiffs' Motion for a Protective Order. Plaintiff shall provide a privilege log of all documents withheld from production under a claim of privilege. The parties are to meet and confer to limit the scope of the documents to be produced on subsequent days of the PMK deposition and plaintiff Grace Polo's deposition and in connection with Mark Simens' deposition. Counsel for Plaintiff and for Defendant shall each bring a proposed protective order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)
